sources.title: Marianne (MGM,1929) MARION DAVIES🍕 ROMANCE MUSICAL🍕
sources.description:
PizzaFLIX Presents CLASSIC MOVIE MONDAY
Stars: Marion Davies, George Baxter, Lawrence Gray
Director: Robert Z. Leonard
🍕MARION DAVIES In Her First Talking Picture🍕
Set during the final days of World War I, the silver screen star captivates as a French farm girl torn between duty and desire. She’s promised to one man fighting on the frontlines… but falls for an American doughboy at home. What’s a girl to do?

Directed by Robert Z. Leonard and co-starring George Baxter and Lawrence Gray, this classic romantic musical comedy from MGM brings the spirit of World War I to life through song, laughter, and heartfelt emotion. 

Are you a PizzaFLIX Fanatic? Join our exclusive Fan Club for dedicated Classic movie lovers: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCE34fwgW7kWr7tc7YiOWtRw/join

🍕This 1929 gem highlights Marion Davies' remarkable transition from silent films to sound, showcasing her charm, comedic timing, and musical abilities. If you love romance, musicals, comedies, and classic love stories set against the backdrop of WWI, Marianne is a must-watch!

Brought to you by PizzaFLIX, your go-to channel for vintage cinema classics, rare MGM treasures, and unforgettable performances. Discover the brilliance of George Baxter, Lawrence Gray, and the visionary direction of Robert Z. Leonard in this beautifully restored piece of film history.

Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more Marion Davies, Golden Age Hollywood, and classic movie magic!

metadata.Plot: During World War I, a French girl is romanced by an American doughboy even though she is promised to a French soldier who is fighting at the front. She falls in love with the Yank however out of her commitment to her French soldier she stays with him especially since he returns from the war blind. Do circumstances change so she can follow him to "Second Forty Street" in New York? Musical numbers include "Just You, Just Me."
metadata.Writer: Dale Van Every, Laurence Stallings, Gladys Unger
metadata.Actors: Marion Davies, George Baxter, Lawrence Gray
